Name: Lane1BridgeClosureAudit

The audited split family admits a source.Method: Exact quotient-coordinate and descended-scalar evaluation on the audited unit representative Junit.
The audited split family admits a source.Acceptance: The explicit split-controlled surrogate family already contains a unit representative whose quotient point is the canonical source-side bridge point.

Any two audited subordinate realizations.Method: Exact affine-family audit on Junit + Aminus . a and Junit + Aminus . b, including accepted-boundary projection.
Any two audited subordinate realizations.Acceptance: On the audited family, uniqueness modulo Aminus holds and all physically visible bridge invariants are unchanged across subordinate realizations.

Finite-cutoff canonical representative r.Method: Exact comparison of two distinct positive calibrations, their Jcan representatives, quotient coordinates, reduced welded images, and accepted welded boundary images.
Finite-cutoff canonical representative r.Acceptance: The canonical-representative step can choose different exact vectors under different positive calibrations, but each remains subordinate to the same source-side bridge point and preserves the same visible images.

Split-threshold alone forces c_Lambda !=.Method: Imported exact threshold counterexample from the delayed descendant reentry audit.
Split-threshold alone forces c_Lambda !=.Acceptance: The nondegeneracy input remains on the separate lane-2 theorem path.

ResidualBoundary: This is an exact finite-dimensional certificate on the current split-controlled surrogate family, not an abstract proof of the full UOM theorem stack.
The theorem-backed note now reduces the live lane-1 boundary to existence of one source-compatible unit descendant realization subordinate to the canonical source-side bridge point.
Accepted welded boundary image invariance is exact-family support here; the theorem-backed note controls the quotient point and reduced welded image.
